definitions.text: From a higher position to a lower one; downwards.
examples: The cat jumped down from the table.

definitions.text: To or towards what is considered the bottom of something, irrespective of whether this is presently physically lower.
examples: Go down to the bottom of the page.
As I lay on my back, a pain shot down from my neck to my waist.

definitions.text: At a lower or further place or position along a set path.
examples: His place is farther down the road.
The company was well down the path to bankruptcy.

definitions.text: From the higher end to the lower of.
examples: The ball rolled down the hill.

definitions.text: From north to south of.
examples: We sailed down the eastern seaboard.

definitions.text: Towards the mouth of (a river); in the direction of flow of.
examples: In this game we float sticks down the river.

definitions.text: Facing downwards.
examples: Turn the cloth over so that the patterned side is down.

definitions.text: At a lower level than before.
examples: The stock market is down.
Prices are down.

definitions.text: Sad, unhappy, depressed, feeling low.
examples: Mary seems very down since she split up with her boyfriend.
Been down so long it seems like up to me

definitions.text: To knock (someone or something) down; to cause to come down; to fell. .mw-parser-output .defdate{font-size:smaller}
examples: The storm downed several old trees along the highway.
A single rifle shot downed the mighty beast.

definitions.text: Specifically, to cause (something in the air) to fall to the ground; to bring down (with a missile etc.).
examples: The helicopter was downed by a surface-to-air missile.

definitions.text: To lower; to put (something) down.
examples: The bell rang for lunch, and the workers downed their tools.

definitions.text: A negative aspect; a downer, a downside.
examples: I love almost everything about my job. The only down is that I can't take Saturdays off.

definitions.text: A grudge (on someone).
A downer, depressant.
A hill; in England, especially a chalk hill.
examples: We went for a walk over the downs.
The North Downs are a ridge of chalk hills in south east England.

definitions.text: A field, especially one used for horse racing.
A tract of poor, sandy, undulating or hilly land near the sea, covered with fine turf which serves chiefly for the grazing of sheep.
Soft, fluffy immature feathers which grow on young birds. Used as insulating material in duvets, sleeping bags and jackets.
To cover, ornament, line, or stuff with down.
